title: Fallout: Create
description: Thrown into a world decimated by nuclear fallout, you must scavenge technology and use the Create mod to build essential machinery.
body:
# Fallout: Create

**Survive. Scavenge. Build. Automate.**

**Welcome to the Radlands**,  a brutal, post-apocalyptic world decimated by nuclear fallout. In **Fallout: Create**, you are not just a survivor; you are an engineer of the rebuild. This modpack merges the intense survival experience of the Fallout universe with the deep, **gear-driven automation of the Create mod**.

![The Power Armor and TaCZ workbench](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/33b70584ace544ba49840ca4b10fd9709411aa81.png)

Fight through a radioactive wasteland to survive, rebuild, and automate your existence in this unique post-apocalyptic World.


## ⚙️ The Gameplay Experience

**Fallout: Create** forces you to see the world in a new way. 

- A Devastated World: Explore a barren landscape filled with ruins, destroyed cities, and radioactive biomes. Standard water is lethal; you must purify or distill it.

- Create Automation in Focus: Your survival hinges on your ability to build machines. Use gears, belts, and steam to construct water filtration systems, automated farms, and defenses. Scrap is gold.

- Radiation is a Threat: A dynamic radiation system punishes carelessness. Find or craft protective gear (Hazmat Suits) to explore the most dangerous zones.

- Weapons and Defense: As the world is populated by dangerous mutants, you will need simple firearms and improvised melee weapons to defend yourself.

###📦 Included Core Mods (Excerpt)
- Create Mod + addons: The heart of automation and engineering.
- Simple Radioactive: Ensures the ever-present threat of contamination.
- Timeless and Classics Zero Mod (TaCZ) + addons: For the necessary firepower.
- Lost Cities: Generates the post-apocalyptic world.
... and many Quality-of-Life mods for a smooth 1.20.1 Forge experience.

**Requirements & Performance**

- Minecraft Version: 1.20.1 (Forge)
- Recommended RAM: At least 6-8 GB of RAM allocated.
- Shaders: We recommend using dark, atmospheric shaders for the best experience.
